skill.name: upstash-qstash
skill.description: Guides implementation of Upstash QStash for serverless message queues, scheduled HTTP jobs, webhook delivery, and signature verification.
skill.summary: Build reliable serverless HTTP queues and scheduled jobs with Upstash QStash.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged backtick-formatted related skill names and weak cryptography keywords. Manual review found these are false positives: the backticks are Markdown formatting, and the weak-crypto matches are ordinary prose. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks in Related Skills
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution on the related skills line. The line only uses Markdown inline code formatting for skill names and does not instruct execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 68 contains only Markdown-formatted related skill names. No shell command, script, or executable code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography on prose lines in the skill description and sharp-edge guidance. The text does not reference DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are natural-language metadata and callback guidance. No cryptographic primitive or insecure algorithm use appears there.

content.value_statement: Serverless applications need reliable background delivery without queue infrastructure. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design QStash queues, schedules, callbacks, and verification flows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use QStash for delayed HTTP delivery and scheduled jobs.
Guides message publishing patterns for endpoints and URL groups.
Covers webhook delivery, callback handling, and failure callback design.
Highlights signature verification as a required production control.
Identifies QStash sharp edges such as private endpoints, retry settings, payload size, and rate limits.
limitations: Does not provision an Upstash account or create QStash resources directly.
Does not include runnable SDK examples in the skill file.
Requires current Upstash documentation for exact plan limits and API changes.
Assumes target endpoints are publicly reachable for QStash delivery.

use_cases.title: Add Reliable Background Jobs
use_cases.description: Design delayed task delivery for a serverless application that cannot run long background workers.

use_cases.title: Create Serverless Cron Workflows
use_cases.description: Plan recurring HTTP jobs with callbacks, failure handling, and retry behavior.

use_cases.title: Harden Webhook Processing
use_cases.description: Review QStash endpoint design for signature verification, fast acknowledgments, and deduplication.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a QStash Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new serverless task flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide whether Upstash QStash fits this workflow: [describe the HTTP task, timing, retries, and endpoint].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Scheduled Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a QStash scheduled HTTP job for [task]. Include endpoint behavior, retry settings, callback handling, and operational checks.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Webhook Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my QStash receiver design for security and reliability. Focus on signature verification, idempotency, response time, and failures.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Production Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user replacing custom queue infrastructure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan from [current queue or cron system] to QStash. Cover message mapping, deduplication, rollbacks, observability, and risk controls.

output_examples.input: A Next.js app needs to send welcome emails five minutes after signup.
output: Use delayed QStash delivery to call a public email endpoint after signup.
Make the endpoint verify signatures, acknowledge quickly, and process the email idempotently.
Add a failure callback so missed sends become visible to operations.

output_examples.input: A team wants a nightly report without running a server.
output: Use a QStash schedule that calls a report generation endpoint at the required time.
Keep the endpoint short and move long work into follow-up messages when needed.
Track failures through callbacks and alerting instead of relying only on retries.

output_examples.input: A webhook receiver sometimes processes the same payment event twice.
output: Add deduplication around the event identifier before changing external state.
Verify QStash signatures before accepting the request.
Return a fast success response after durable validation and enqueue slower work separately.

best_practices: Verify QStash webhook signatures on every production receiver.
Design endpoints to acknowledge quickly and move slow work into separate tasks.
Use deduplication and callbacks for business-critical message flows.
anti_patterns: Do not expose QStash receivers without signature verification.
Do not send large payloads when a stable reference can identify the work.
Do not assume QStash can call private, localhost, or development-only endpoints.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps plan Upstash QStash messaging, scheduled HTTP calls, webhooks, callbacks, retries, and production safeguards.

faq.question: Can it create QStash resources automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and review support, but it does not access your Upstash account.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes signature verification, endpoint design, deduplication, and failure handling.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this suitable for private endpoints?
faq.answer: No. QStash delivery requires reachable HTTP endpoints, so private or localhost targets need another design.

faq.question: Should I still check official Upstash documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Use current Upstash documentation for exact API behavior, plan limits, and pricing details.
